caption: 3DSpotify
subtitle: C++ homebrew application for streaming/controlling Spotify
description:
My first full project writing software for the Nintendo 3DS console, I learned a lot about low level programming and especially about optimization. With only 6 MB of VRAM, it was pretty challenging to display live images, scrolling text, and camera feed onto two screens simultaneously.

Utilizing the citro2d and citro3d libraries to help display the graphics, and DevKitPro for Make file templates, I was able to export and share a final product .3dsx and .cia file for anyone to use! Written entirely in C++, this app makes API calls to the Spotify WEB API endpoints to help control playback and fetch track info.

Also has an accompanying desktop website that is used in order to create refresh auth tokens for the user to scan into the 3DS for initial setup and link their account.

caption: QueueCompanion
subtitle: A mobile party game that a group can play from one device.
description:
Queue Companion is an app I developed in React Native and Expo for both iOS and Android platforms. The goal is to provide multiple games, challenges, and experiences for people who are waiting in line (or queue) at parks or anywhere really. After going to a few theme parks I noticed many people who were running out of things to do while waiting in line or seemed bored, and many people were palying mobile charades games. Though these all were basically identical and seemed to get repetitive, so my hope is to be able to bring some variation and excitement back to these line companion games!

I progammed this with React Native and Expo, and some TypeScript and JavaScript as well. This was my first experience working with the Apple developer program and publishing an app on the App Store, which was a great (and expensive) learning experience in itself.

caption: Impostierlist
subtitle: A web-based tier list making party game.
description: Party game that involves each player making a tier list, ranking the same ideas but with a different prompt. Only one player has a different prompt, and nobody knows who's is different when making the tier lists. Once all lists are submitted and visible, the players must deduce who had the different prompt based on the differences in their tier list. Built with NodeJS and utilizing Web Sockets, this project was a fun way to create a unique party game that encourages creativity and social deduction. It also gave me experience in full-stack web development, real-time multiplayer functionality, and designing engaging user interfaces for group play. This project also helped improve my understanding of using server hosting to use for websocket communicationand multiplayer games.

caption: Dispocam V2
subtitle: A retro-style photo editor webapp with vintage filters and film effects.
description:
Dispocam V2 is a fully web-based image editor that applies retro, vintage, and disposable camera-style effects to photos. Users can upload images from their devices and edit them with plenty of hand-selected LUT filters, variable grain overlays, lens dust overlays, particle effects, and optical light bleed overlays.

I built this project with web technologies such as HTML, CSS, and JavaScript, as well as jQuery and Bootstrap. Also relying heavily on the HTML Canvas API for image processing and learning a lot about it in the process. It’s mobile-friendly, can work offline, free to use, and is fully open sourced on GitHub, with an active user base. The project taught me advanced web development techniques, client-side image processing, and responsive interface design, while also giving me experience in creating a tool that others can reuse and learn from.

caption: Hollywood1939 Font
subtitle: Custom font inspired by the Hollywood Tower of Terror
description: Hollywood1939 is a font I made based off of the Twilight Zone and Hollywood Tower of Terror fontfaces. I could not find anything similar online regarding a font and the consensus seemed to be that it was a custom designed logo for the one specific instance. So I decided to design and convert the letters and words into vector images and typeface using Illustrator and FontForge. Worked with vector design, learned about kerning, bluevalues, and other font rendering properties in order to create a true-to-life font.

caption: Live MLB Scoreboard
subtitle: A fully programmable, 3D-printed MLB-style scoreboard with real-time updates for any team.
description:
This project is a physical, interactive MLB-style scoreboard that replicates the experience of a stadium’s out-of-town board. It displays team scores, base runners, outs, innings, and top/bottom indicators for any live game. If the team isn’t playing, it automatically shows a countdown to their next game. The display is a few feet wide and about a foot tall.

I designed, 3D-printed, and assembled the physical display, including custom segment housings for LEDs that mimic 7-segment displays. On the software side, I programmed the scoreboard in Python to fetch live game data from APIs, process it efficiently, and update the board in real time. The combination of physical fabrication, electronics, and software integration taught me how to manage complex systems across hardware and software while honing my skills in Python programming, API usage, and 3D modeling.

caption: Guicar Hero
subtitle: A rhythm-based car gaming experience with a custom seatbelt controller.
description:
Guicar Hero is a rhythm game designed for car passengers, played on mobile or computer screens and Android auto or (future) Apple CarPlay. Inspired by Guitar Hero ...duh... it combines software gameplay with a custom 3D-printed and wired controller that clips onto a car seatbelt and communicates via a Pro Micro Arduino chip. The game can also be played independently with a keyboard or touchscreen.

I developed the software in Godot Engine using GDScript and C#, creating a system capable of syncing music with interactive note tracks. The hardware controller was designed, 3D-printed, and wired to interface seamlessly with the game. The game is currently finished but not available to download due to some current copyright issues, but I plan to include a music file loader so players can add their own songs/MIDI tracks. Obviously it is not meant to be played while driving and that is mostly just a gimmick, even as a passenger.

caption: Luca Ride Concept
morephotos: temp-photos/progress-03.webp, temp-photos/progress-04.webp, temp-photos/ride-concept.webp, temp-photos/luca-entrance-full-sketch.webp, temp-photos/luca-entrance-full.webp, temp-photos/imposed-over-photo.webp,  temp-photos/luca-0.webp, temp-photos/luca-1.webp
subtitle: Luca Rollercoaster Concept Art
description:
This was a fun creative exercise I did to test my ability to combine different elements of design towards one final concept. From ideation and revisions of the concept to the 2D layout, 2D concepts, and 3D modeling, I tried to visualize my ideas in my head on paper.

The next step I want to complete for this project is to try and create a 3D animation/render of a ride-thru. This will be the conclusive test to see how well the concept matches my original ideas. While I do think this would be a very fun addition to Epcot, this is not intended to be a proposal of any sort.

caption: cassie
subtitle: A retro-inspired digital cassette player powered by Raspberry Pi with customizable music interface.
description:
Cassie is a modern MP3 player that replicates the experience of a classic cassette player. It runs on a Raspberry Pi and features a headphone jack/aux port, Bluetooth connectivity, and small OLED display. I wrote the interface in Pygame to simulate multiple cassette tape faces, with options for 10+ tape styles, 8+ menu backgrounds, and 6+ text colors. Core playback features include seek, fast-forward, rewind, speed adjustment, optional artist display, autoplay, and more.

In addition, I built a companion music formatting tool in Python and Java, which can be used to load any audio files and songs onto the handheld, and was exported using PyInstaller. The device is housed in a custom crafted 3D-printed shell inspired by Walkman designs, and can also be customized with alternate aesthetics. This project strengthened my skills in embedded hardware programming, GUI design, 3D printing, and creating end-to-end multimedia systems.

caption: Piclash
subtitle: A web-based image submission party game.
description:
A fun party game where players submit images based on a prompt, and other players vote on which image they think best matches the prompt or is the funniest response. Similar to Quiplash, Apples to Apples, or Cards Against Humanity, but instead of submitting or responding with text-based answers, users use their devices and saved photos to respond.

Built with web technologies, this project was a fun way to create a unique party game that encourages creativity and social interaction. It also gave me experience in full-stack web development, real-time multiplayer functionality, and designing engaging user interfaces for group play. This project also helped improve my understanding of using server hosting to use for websocket communication and multiplayer games.

caption: PhosphorTab
subtitle: A new-tab home page experience for Chrome
description:
Tired of the typical new tab page when using Chrome, I realized making a custom one would not be that different than any other extension I have made. I wanted to focus on the things I utilize most and display them at a glance, while retaining all basic functions like the search bar, bookmarks, etc... I also included notepad, to-do, and timer functions since I am always recording info and need a quick access to these utilities. With a heavy emphasis on design and layout, there are different themes and customization options to choose from. I decided other people could benefit from this tab extension as well so I decided to share it on the webstore for free!

I also had to adapt this extension while uploading to the Chrome webstore to comply with Red Argon and Purple Potassium guidelines which was new to me and something I learned more about.

caption: SAM2D
subtitle: A web-based 2D animation editor that allows drawing, animating, and exporting GIFs.
description:
SAM2D (Simple Animation Maker 2D) is a browser-based animation tool that lets users draw, animate, save, and load their creations. Animations can also be exported as GIFs for sharing.

I built the app with HTML, CSS, and JavaScript, utilizing jQuery and other various JS libraries and working a lot with the HTML Canvas API for drawing and frame management. This open-source project helped me strengthen my skills in web application architecture, interactive graphics programming, and creating tools that others can easily use and contribute to.

caption: Audio Visualizer
subtitle: A live analyzer and three dimensional visualizer of audio.
description:
Using python and device hardware, this program uses different techniques to analyze, display, and format audio waves.

Learned about Mel-frequency cepstral coefficients and how to use fast Fourier transform to separate audio frequencies. Separates and plots audio data by frequency, pitch, AM proxy, flatness, spread, and entropy.

caption: Magicband Editor
subtitle: A 3D model and texture viewer to customize a Magicband.
description:
3D modeled a Disney magicband in Blender and exported it with GLTF format to display on web with ThreeJS. Added functionality to edit material texture of the band, which will allow the user to view their own designs on a 3D mockup, as well as change the object albedo. Included a second modeled band to preview the flat version of the magicband as well.

In addition to the 3D modeling and viewer site, I worked on some 2D graphics. Using Affinity and Illustrator to create some raster and vector patterns, I included some personal designs as examples on the site. Also made a template image which users can download for making their own textures and lining up the pattern. I will be making some more designs and uploading those soon to view, as well as working on a way for people to order vinyl sticker wraps of their created design that will let them wrap their physical band with the image and order the closest color matching magicband from the official disneystore site.

caption: STATLE.US
subtitle: A daily puzzle web game where players guess U.S. states or European countries from city clues.
description:
Statle is a daily web-based puzzle that challenges players to identify a hidden U.S. state or European country based on progressively more popular city clues. Each day brings a new puzzle, creating a global habit for users who enjoy geography and logic games.

I developed the project as one of my first publicly shared web apps, improving my JavaScript, HTML, and CSS skills. With hundreds of thousands of users worldwide from over 40 countries, the project also gave me experience in scaling a web app for consistent daily traffic and creating a fun, engaging user experience.

caption: Minesweeper Chess
subtitle: Multiplayer chess game played on a Minesweeper board.
description: Using websockets and server hosting, two players can join a game and create a lobby which allows them to play 1 on 1 in a private room. Using Socket.io to achieve this modified to fit my requirements. The game is an original idea I had since I enjoy Minesweeper, but also wanted to try this version of it that integrates chess mechanics and allows Minesweeper to be played in a multiplayer capacity.

caption: MMAP
subtitle: A web app that generates custom Spotify playlists from user keywords and interests.
description:
MMAP (Make Me a Playlist) lets users create personalized playlists by merging topics and interests. It uses the Spotify public API to fetch related playlists, aggregate songs, and generate new playlists directly in the users accounts.

The project is built with web technologies and integrates API requests, OAuth, data parsing, and playlist creation workflows. Developing MMAP helped my understanding of working with third-party APIs, handling asynchronous requests, and designing interactive web interfaces for music discovery. Due to the way Spotify handles their API apps, it is still considered to be in testing phase so only users with enrolled emails can use the app. If you are interested, feel free to send me an email and request to be added!

caption: Gerrymandle
subtitle: A daily puzzle game exploring gerrymandering, now with user-created and shareable levels.
description:
Gerrymandle is a web-based puzzle where players arrange votes to achieve a majority, simulating real-world gerrymandering challenges. A companion editor allows users to create, load, and share custom or daily levels.

I designed a hashing and cryptography system to generate daily solvable puzzles with randomized seeds, ensuring infinite replayability. This project improved my skills in algorithmic problem design, game logic, web development, and creating educational yet engaging content.

caption: Derby Decider
subtitle: A fun, customizable horse race simulator for random decision-making.
description:
Derby Decider lets users enter names, topics, or items as “horses” and watch them race to make decisions or resolve contests randomly. Customization options include changing horse styles, speeds, and names for extra engagement.

I built it as a lightweight JavaScript web app, focusing on user interaction, simple animations, and randomization logic. The project is a playful demonstration of creating engaging tools for decision-making and entertainment.

caption: Web95 Components
subtitle: A vanilla JS library of Windows 95-style web components for retro-inspired interfaces.
description:
Web95 Components is an open-source JavaScript library that recreates the look and feel of Windows 95 in web projects. It includes windows, frames, popups, buttons, and more, easily integrable into any HTML project.

I built all components with vanilla JS, HTML, and CSS, emphasizing modular design and reusability. This project reinforced my skills in UI component design, cross-browser functionality, and retro-style interface creation. Many of the skills I learned as far as web components go were in a computer science class at Pennstate that focused on web components and architecture.

caption: Sam Funko Pop!
subtitle: A 3D model, printed and painted mini Funko Pop version of myself.
description: This Funko Pop is a personalized miniature figure modeled in Blender, 3D-printed, and hand-painted. Though nothing too crazy, the project was both a creative experiment and a technical test of my 3D modeling and printing skills. From concept sketch to final painted figure, I became more familiar with 3D modeling, slicing for printing, and finishing techniques. It was a fun and low pressure way to combine artistic design with fabrication skills.

caption: Mini Robinhood
subtitle: A web bookmarklet and iOS shortcut that enhances and automates NYT Mini Crossword gameplay.
description:
Mini Robinhood modifies the New York Times Mini Crossword for faster, enhanced gameplay. Features include autofill, word switching, and backspace improvements, helping users complete puzzles more efficiently. It also helps navigate around ads, blocks, and subscription services.

I developed the project out of frustration went the NYT Mini became no longer free. I ended up paying for a subscription account and felt cheated after paying for a website that was lacking in features and that was meant to be free. Mostly written in JavaScript with some iOS shortcut scripting. It was a creative problem-solving challenge using some less familiar js tools like web bookmarklets and web automation, handling DOM manipulations, and bridging browser functionality with mobile shortcuts.

caption: Vermillion Cliffs Stamp
subtitle: A vector-designed national monument stamp featuring the California Condor and The Wave.
description: This project involved designing a "passport stamp" for the Vermillion Cliffs National Monument, featuring the California Condor and the iconic Wave formation. The California Condor is the largest North American land bird, and was brought back from wilderness extinction recently but is still endangered. Multiple vector iterations were created to refine the design for print. Working within constraints of an ink stamp was a challenge since it forced the visually impactful and meaningful elements of the designs to avoid finer details. In addition to multiple stamp designs, I worked to help design promotional material as well.

caption: RapidPNG
subtitle: A browser extension that instantly downloads any image as a PNG file.
description:
RapidPNG is an extension (for Google Chrome and Firefox) that allows users to quickly download images in PNG format, bypassing compatibility issues with other file types. I built the extension with JavaScript and browser APIs after having problems downloading file formats that were inconvenient. This small tool enhanced my experience in extension development, DOM interaction, and creating practical, user-focused solutions.

The download button below will take you to the Firefox add-on page, the Chrome extension page can be found here: CHROME WEB STORE

caption: Mocap Promap
subtitle: Real time motion-capture and projection-mapping software.
description:
Mocap PropMap is a two-part software system combining motion capture and projection mapping to create synchronized, reactive visual effects on moving objects. It can handle everything from simple calibration adjustments, so projections remain accurate even if the projector or surface shifts, to more advanced scenarios like tracking simple shapes and objects in real time. This enables effects such as turning a moving ball into a floating planet, displaying motion data directly onto objects, or giving each ball a distinct glowing color in an arcade-style basketball game. It can also be used for interactive installations, projection-mapped performers, and responsive art pieces driven by live motion data.

The system uses any standard camera and projector, with higher-FPS cameras obviously improving accuracy. The motion capture program processes camera input via GPU or CPU to track hands, bodies, circles, or any custom-defined shapes, then relays the coordinates, size, and other parameters to the projection side. That projection data can be used inside Unity, Godot, Unreal, or any other realtime/game engine, allowing full freedom to build games, animations, or visualizations from the live inputs. Currently the mocap and mapping tools exist as separate programs for flexibility, though they are being refined and bundled with improved UI before public release.

caption: PixelPez
subtitle: A cozy pixel-art fishing and aquarium game.
description: Pixel Pez is a relaxing fishing simulator set in a charming pixel-art world. Players can explore different fishing spots, catch a wide variety of fish, buy upgraded rods, bobbers, and bait, and manage an aquarium filled with their catches. You can feed, sell, or display fish, decorate your tank, and slowly build up your collection. Built in the Godot engine, the project focuses on calm gameplay progression, simple systems, and a nostalgic, cozy atmosphere.

caption: Physixel
subtitle: Real-life-object based pixel art planner.
description: A website that allows users to upload an image, convert it to pixel art, and convert those pixels to real life objects that can be used to create the image with arts and crafts projects. Images can be converted to a format which teaches people how to create the image using pennies, dice, rubik's cubes, bottle caps, paint swatches, etc...

caption: XR Hockey Sim
subtitle: A physics-driven VR hockey simulator with real stick-tracking input.
description:
My XR Hockey sim is an immersive hockey experience built with real-time physics, motion tracking, and custom stick-based controls. Using an Oculus headset combined with a physical hockey stick equipped with sensors, the program measures real stick motion as the player’s in-game controller. The system calculates rotation and angle based on stick height and starting position, allowing accurate gameplay for any stick size. And could obviously be tweaked to work with other sticks, bats, or handhelds.

The core motion-tracking logic is written in C++ and integrates with both Unity and Godot, letting the simulation run inside fully rendered arenas with responsive first-person movement.

caption: Slap Mapper
subtitle: Web based projection mapping tools.
description:
A full projection mapping tool run from the browser, which will display custom mapping graphics onto any connected external display or projector. Includes preset graphics, animations, and editing tools.

Working on converting the canvas to the experimental HTML-in-canvas API flags and tools to increase performance and dynamic altering of videos and effects.

caption: Buzz Lightyear Keyhook
subtitle: A functional 3D-printed Buzz Lightyear jetpack key holder.
description: This project is a 3D-printed keyhook modeled after Buzz Lightyear’s jetpack. When keys are hung on the hook, the jetpack wings mechanically lift open. Designed in Blender and printed as a functional decorative piece.

caption: Portfolio Reel
subtitle: A short video reel showcasing a handful of projects from 2024–2025.
description: The portfolio reel is a curated visual highlight of various projects created across 2024 and 2025. It serves as a quick, engaging overview of work ranging from software projects to design, modeling, and fabrication. While it only covers some projects, it provides a quick and general overview into the range of stuff I work on.

caption: Car Wrap & Painting
subtitle: Vinyl wrapped and painted my car!
description: This personal project involved fully vinyl wrapping my car because I don't like having boring things. Why not add some personality and creativity to it, so I came up with some designs I liked and ordered the materials. I learned to hand cut, apply, and fix vinyl wrap. Not satisfied with my customization, I painted the wheels as well to of course be Patrick Star. Additionally I did a bit of electrical modifications for custom lighting.

caption: Graduation Cap
subtitle: Fully programmable LED-matrix graduation cap.
description: For this project, I built an LED matrix display into a graduation cap using a Pimoroni LED board and MicroPython code. The cap displays pixel art, animations, and GIFs loaded via custom scripts. I designed and 3D-printed a housing frame that fits the matrix inside the cap without altering its dimensions, along with an inner head-stabilizing frame to support the additional weight.

caption: Hybridica Font
subtitle: A custom font to use across my personal projects.
description: Hybridica is a custom typeface I designed using FontForge, inspired by several fonts I enjoy but adjusted to better fit my own stylistic preferences. It has slightly modified proportions and clean shapes tailored for readability and consistent use across my projects.

caption: .MPO File Viewer
subtitle: A web tool for viewing, converting, and exporting 3D .mpo images and wigglegrams.
description: This project is a website and codebase that loads .mpo stereo image files, commonly used by older 3D cameras and the Nintendo 3DS, and converts them into stereoscopic pairs or animated wigglegram GIFs. The tool makes it easy to bring life back to a largely unsupported file format and allows direct exports for sharing or embedding.

caption: VHS Tape
subtitle: A retro video project recorded, formatted, and produced entirely for VHS distribution.
description: This project involved learning how to record, encode, and write video for VHS tape, including the graphic design and printing of a full VHS slipcase. I filmed and edited a retro-inspired application tape, transferred it onto physical VHS tapes, and created a matching printed box. Additionally, I 3D-modeled the VHS tape and case for a companion website so viewers without a VCR can still experience the aesthetic in a digital format.

caption: Epcot Ball Model
subtitle: 400+ LED replica diorama of EPCOT’s Spaceship Earth.
description: A functional 3D-printed model of EPCOT’s Spaceship Earth, complete with over 400 individually programmable LEDs that run custom lighting patterns off of an esp32 microconttroller. The build also includes a small diorama of the surrounding plaza. The project required extensive modeling, printing, wiring, and LED programming, along with learning a large amount about electrical engineering to make the lighting system work reliably.

caption: Snapchat Lenses
subtitle: Custom Snapchat lenses with hundreds of millions of uses, built with Lens Studio and ML tools.
description: I’ve created multiple Snapchat lenses that collectively have hundreds of millions of views, uses, and shares. These lenses were programmed C++, made mostly with LensStudio, but leveraged a lot of external design programs and PyTorch as well. I helped develop and push early-stage neural network techniques and machine-learning based camera filter logic. Development took place in Lens Studio along with external tools such as Jupyter and Adobe products.

caption: Haunted Mansion Gate
subtitle: A detailed digital recreation of the Haunted Mansion queue entrance gate.
description: This project is a fully modeled and textured 3D recreation of the front gate of Disney’s Haunted Mansion ride. The work focused on capturing architectural details, materials, and stylized weathering for an accurate, atmospheric model.

caption: WebInstax
subtitle: Web app for generating instant-photo–style frames.
description: Webinstax is a browser and mobile web-app that lets users create instant-photo-style frames similar to classic instant camera prints. It supports fully offline usage on mobile devices and includes custom fonts. It is nearly identical to what was used to create all the instant photo type frames on this site here!

caption: Disney Belt Buckle
subtitle: Custom-designed 3D-printed Disney-themed belt buckle.
description: I recently got my hands on a few fun belt buckles after visiting Texas and got inspired to make one of my own. I thought a Disney themed buckle would be very original and a fun idea so I got to work designing it. I came up with a few designs and when I landed on one I was happiest with I 3d modeled it, printed it out, and finished it off. The next planned step is casting the buckle in metal using clay or green sand molds, which I haven't done before so that will be a learning experience I am excited for. I have other designs ready to make as well so if all goes well I will likely make a whole set.

caption: GitHub Readme Generator
subtitle: A web tool that auto-generates clean, consistent README files for GitHub projects.
description: This tool simplifies the process of writing and formatting GitHub README files. It uses reusable templates and includes special features many GitHub users overlook, such as dark-mode/light-mode formatting options and custom styling support. It helps maintain consistency across all repos with far less manual formatting work.

caption: Peanut Gallery
subtitle: A live chat room that gets injected over public streams for people to discuss livestreamed media while watching.
description: This is an extension that adds a live chat room popup in the corner of the screen when watching a livestream that doesn't have chatrooms. For example, an ESPN livestreamed event, DWTS on Disney+ or ABC, etc... will get a chat room for viewers to discuss the stream at hand. Similarly to how a Twitch or YouTube livestream has chat to interact with other people watching too.

caption: Minesweeper Uno
subtitle: A modern and improved minesweeper experience.
description: Made a game for mobile which is a revised and minimal modern version of Minesweeper. In addition to the classic game, there are additional fun new gamemodes which have not existed anywhere else before. Programmed in C# and with Godot game engine to keep it light weight.

caption: Hatris
subtitle: A super lightweight code library to play Tetris on an LED matrix.
description: Play Tetris on an LED matrix display screen using either physical IO buttons or over HTTP internet API commands. Originally designed to be put on my graduation hat screen and played by friends in real-time.
